political.s: Old Dominion made $88K in verified FEC political donations with a strong right-lean (100% Republican allocation). The company is union-free, which aligns with conservative labor policies.

charity.s: Old Dominion demonstrates consistent charitable engagement, with $250K annual pledges to American Red Cross Disaster Relief, 25+ years supporting United Way, and donations to military-focused causes like Folds of Honor. Community giving appears genuine though specific annual figures beyond Red Cross pledge are not disclosed.
charity.focus: Disaster relief, United Way, military families, youth sports

environment.s: Old Dominion reports 12% reduction in scope 1&2 emissions (2018-2020) and has piloted battery-electric equipment. However, no science-based emissions targets established, and the company explicitly states viable diesel Class 8 tractor replacements do not exist in the market, limiting climate action.

labor.s: As an explicitly union-free company with 20K+ employees, Old Dominion maintains non-unionized workforce while reporting 90%+ driver retention and 95% home-daily return rates. However, NLRB cases exist with settlement history. Average employee salary ~$89K vs. CEO pay of $12.6M (163:1 ratio).

dei.s: Old Dominion states commitment to equal opportunity employment and has supervisor/management diversity training programs promoting internal advancement. Women in Trucking Silver Partnership since 2017. No anti-DEI positions identified but limited DEI disclosure in public materials.

animals.s: Not applicable to trucking and freight logistics operations.

guns.s: Not applicable to trucking and freight logistics operations.

privacy.s: Limited public information on data privacy practices. Company operates driver tracking and digital freight systems but no comprehensive privacy policy disclosures found in public materials.

execPay.s: CEO Kevin Freeman earned $12.62M in 2024 (92.2% performance-based), representing 163:1 ratio to median employee earning $77.4K. Incentive payouts declined 12.6% in 2025 due to lower profitability. Pay structure heavily weighted toward bonuses tied to pre-tax income metrics.
